transferList.to: Designing experiences through space → designing experiences through interfaces
Precision and clarity → Building thoughtful design systems

name: Koh Mei Qi
aboutParagraphs: Over the past few years, I've worked in architecture, designing and coordinating complex projects where clarity, usability, and human experience matter just as much as aesthetics. My background has taught me to think in systems, navigate constraints, and translate complexity into experiences that people can intuitively understand and use.
I believe every designed experience — whether a building, an interface, or an object — reflects a way of thinking about people.

My work and interests are shaped by curiosity: how people make decisions, how environments influence behavior, and how complexity can be transformed into something intuitive. I'm drawn to the tension between logic and emotion, structure and spontaneity, precision and ambiguity.
This space is less a portfolio and more an ongoing record of exploration — a collection of questions, experiments, and ideas that continue to shape how I think about design.
heroLede: I'm Koh Mei Qi — a former architectural designer moving into UX/UI design. Years of designing spaces, systems, and experiences for people have shaped how I think about usability, clarity, and human-centered design. Today, I'm applying that same mindset to digital products — creating experiences that are intuitive, meaningful, and thoughtfully crafted.
